peptides.name: BPC-157
peptides.shortSummary: Frequently discussed in relation to tissue recovery and gut comfort.
peptides.overview: BPC-157 is a synthetic peptide that appears in research discussions about soft-tissue recovery and digestive comfort. It is studied largely in preclinical settings.
peptides.mechanism: This peptide is frequently discussed in relation to angiogenesis (new blood vessel formation) and connective-tissue signaling in research literature.

sources.title: Stable gastric pentadecapeptide BPC-157: experimental overview
sources.summary: A narrative review summarizing preclinical experiments exploring tissue-healing pathways. Not human clinical evidence.

sources.title: Community recovery notes (summarized)
sources.summary: Aggregated, anonymized community discussion themes around perceived recovery. Anecdotal and unverified.

peptides.name: Ipamorelin
peptides.shortSummary: Often discussed in relation to sleep quality and recovery in research conversations.
peptides.overview: Ipamorelin is a growth-hormone-secretagogue peptide commonly referenced in research discussions about sleep and recovery.
peptides.mechanism: Frequently discussed in relation to the ghrelin/GH pathway in published research.

sources.title: Growth hormone secretagogues: a clinical review
sources.summary: Reviews the GH-secretagogue class broadly. General context, not a recommendation.

peptides.name: CJC-1295
peptides.shortSummary: Discussed alongside Ipamorelin in research about recovery and body composition.
peptides.overview: CJC-1295 is a peptide referenced in research discussions about the growth-hormone axis, often mentioned together with Ipamorelin.
peptides.mechanism: Frequently discussed in relation to GHRH analog activity in research literature.

sources.title: GHRH analogs: mechanistic overview
sources.summary: Preclinical mechanistic discussion. Not human clinical data.

peptides.name: Semax
peptides.shortSummary: Frequently discussed in relation to focus and cognitive research.
peptides.overview: Semax is a peptide studied largely in regional research literature and discussed in relation to focus and cognition.
peptides.mechanism: Frequently discussed in relation to BDNF expression in research contexts.

sources.title: Neuropeptide research: regional clinical literature
sources.summary: Summarized regional clinical research. Methodology varies; interpret with caution.

peptides.name: Thymosin Beta-4
peptides.shortSummary: Referenced in research discussions about tissue repair and flexibility.
peptides.overview: Thymosin Beta-4 is a naturally occurring peptide referenced in research about cell migration and tissue repair.
peptides.mechanism: Frequently discussed in relation to actin regulation and cell migration in research.

sources.title: Thymosin beta-4 in tissue repair (review)
sources.summary: Reviews preclinical repair pathways. Not clinical guidance.

peptides.name: GHK-Cu
peptides.shortSummary: Commonly discussed in relation to skin and aging research.
peptides.overview: GHK-Cu is a copper-binding peptide widely referenced in skin and cosmetic research discussions.
peptides.mechanism: Frequently discussed in relation to collagen signaling and antioxidant pathways.

sources.title: Copper peptides in dermatologic research
sources.summary: Overview of cosmetic and dermatologic research themes.

peptides.name: Tirzepatide
peptides.shortSummary: A dual GIP/GLP-1 receptor agonist widely discussed in weight and metabolic research.
peptides.overview: Tirzepatide is an FDA-approved dual GIP/GLP-1 receptor agonist heavily referenced in clinical research on body weight and glucose metabolism. It is a prescription medication.
peptides.mechanism: Frequently discussed in relation to dual incretin (GIP + GLP-1) receptor activity in clinical literature.

sources.title: Tirzepatide in weight management (SURMOUNT program)
sources.summary: Large randomized clinical trials studied tirzepatide for body-weight outcomes. Prescription medication — discuss with a clinician.

peptides.name: Retatrutide
peptides.shortSummary: An investigational triple GIP/GLP-1/glucagon agonist discussed in weight-loss research.
peptides.overview: Retatrutide is an investigational triple receptor agonist (GIP, GLP-1, glucagon) studied in clinical trials for body weight. It is not FDA-approved.
peptides.mechanism: Frequently discussed in relation to triple incretin/glucagon receptor activity in trial literature.

sources.title: Retatrutide phase 2 obesity trial
sources.summary: A phase 2 randomized trial evaluated retatrutide for body-weight change. Investigational — not approved.

peptides.name: Semaglutide
peptides.shortSummary: A GLP-1 receptor agonist widely referenced in weight and glucose research.
peptides.overview: Semaglutide is an FDA-approved GLP-1 receptor agonist extensively studied in clinical research on body weight and type 2 diabetes. It is a prescription medication.
peptides.mechanism: Frequently discussed in relation to GLP-1 receptor activation in clinical literature.

sources.title: Semaglutide for weight management (STEP program)
sources.summary: Randomized clinical trials studied semaglutide for body-weight outcomes. Prescription medication.

peptides.name: Tesamorelin
peptides.shortSummary: A GHRH analog discussed in research on visceral fat and body composition.
peptides.overview: Tesamorelin is an FDA-approved GHRH analog referenced in clinical research on body composition. It is a prescription medication.
peptides.mechanism: Frequently discussed in relation to GHRH-receptor activity and the GH/IGF-1 axis.

sources.title: Tesamorelin and visceral adiposity (clinical)
sources.summary: Clinical trials studied tesamorelin for body-composition endpoints. Prescription medication.

peptides.name: AOD-9604
peptides.shortSummary: A growth-hormone fragment discussed in fat-metabolism research.
peptides.overview: AOD-9604 is a fragment of growth hormone referenced in preclinical research discussions about fat metabolism.
peptides.mechanism: Frequently discussed in relation to lipolysis pathways in preclinical literature.

sources.title: AOD-9604 lipolysis research (preclinical)
sources.summary: Preclinical studies explored fat-metabolism signaling. Human evidence is limited.

peptides.name: MOTS-c
peptides.shortSummary: A mitochondrial-derived peptide discussed in energy and metabolic research.
peptides.overview: MOTS-c is a mitochondrial-derived peptide studied in preclinical research on metabolism and exercise physiology.
peptides.mechanism: Frequently discussed in relation to AMPK signaling and metabolic regulation in research literature.

sources.title: MOTS-c metabolic regulation (preclinical)
sources.summary: Preclinical studies explored metabolic signaling. Not human clinical data.

peptides.name: TB-500
peptides.shortSummary: A synthetic fragment discussed alongside Thymosin Beta-4 in recovery research.
peptides.overview: TB-500 is a synthetic peptide related to Thymosin Beta-4, referenced in preclinical discussions of tissue repair and flexibility.
peptides.mechanism: Frequently discussed in relation to actin regulation and cell migration in preclinical literature.

sources.title: Thymosin Beta-4 and tissue repair (review)
sources.summary: Preclinical review of repair-related pathways. Not human clinical evidence.

peptides.name: Sermorelin
peptides.shortSummary: A GHRH peptide discussed in research on sleep, recovery and the GH axis.
peptides.overview: Sermorelin is a GHRH-analog peptide referenced in research discussions about the growth-hormone axis, sleep and recovery.
peptides.mechanism: Frequently discussed in relation to GHRH-receptor activity in research literature.

sources.title: GHRH analogs overview (review)
sources.summary: General review of the GHRH-analog class. Context, not a recommendation.

peptides.name: MK-677
peptides.shortSummary: An oral GH secretagogue discussed in sleep, recovery and appetite research.
peptides.overview: MK-677 (ibutamoren) is an orally active growth-hormone secretagogue referenced in research about the GH axis, sleep and appetite. It is a non-peptide compound often grouped with peptide research.
peptides.mechanism: Frequently discussed in relation to ghrelin-receptor activity and GH release in research literature.

sources.title: Ghrelin mimetics and GH secretion (review)
sources.summary: Reviews GH-secretagogue research broadly. Context, not a recommendation.

peptides.name: Selank
peptides.shortSummary: A peptide discussed in research on focus, calm and cognition.
peptides.overview: Selank is a synthetic peptide referenced in preclinical research discussions about cognition and stress signaling.
peptides.mechanism: Frequently discussed in relation to tuftsin-related and neuropeptide pathways in preclinical literature.

sources.title: Selank neuropeptide research (preclinical)
sources.summary: Preclinical studies explored cognition-related pathways. Not human clinical data.

peptides.name: Epitalon
peptides.shortSummary: A peptide discussed in longevity and skin-aging research.
peptides.overview: Epitalon is a synthetic peptide referenced in preclinical research discussions about aging biology and telomere science.
peptides.mechanism: Frequently discussed in relation to telomerase and pineal/circadian signaling in preclinical literature.

sources.title: Epitalon and aging biology (preclinical)
sources.summary: Preclinical and small studies explored aging-related markers. Not robust human clinical evidence.

peptides.name: KPV
peptides.shortSummary: A short peptide discussed in gut-comfort and inflammation research.
peptides.overview: KPV is a tripeptide referenced in preclinical research discussions about gut comfort and inflammatory signaling.
peptides.mechanism: Frequently discussed in relation to anti-inflammatory melanocortin pathways in preclinical literature.

sources.title: KPV anti-inflammatory research (preclinical)
sources.summary: Preclinical studies explored inflammatory signaling. Not human clinical data.

peptides.name: Thymosin Alpha-1
peptides.shortSummary: A peptide discussed in immune-support research.
peptides.overview: Thymosin Alpha-1 is a peptide referenced in clinical research discussions about immune modulation; it is approved in some countries.
peptides.mechanism: Frequently discussed in relation to T-cell and immune signaling in research literature.

sources.title: Thymosin Alpha-1 immune research (review)
sources.summary: Reviews immune-modulation research. Regulatory status varies by country.

peptides.name: DSIP
peptides.shortSummary: A peptide discussed in sleep-related research.
peptides.overview: DSIP is a naturally occurring peptide referenced in preclinical research discussions about sleep regulation.
peptides.mechanism: Frequently discussed in relation to sleep-regulatory and neuroendocrine pathways in preclinical literature.

sources.title: DSIP sleep research (preclinical)
sources.summary: Preclinical studies explored sleep-related signaling. Human evidence is limited.

news.summary: Peptide research findings and FDA / regulatory updates are refreshed here daily. General research only. This is not medical advice.
